paragraphs.body: Jan 31, 2025 Have you ever wondered if you were reincarnated? You know like in your past life you were a princess living in a big castle, and now you’re a student in college or maybe you were a dog before and now you’re a human! Well, there is an essay written by Will Franks who proves to you that reincarnation is real by using Dr. Ian Stevenson’s research. There isn’t much information on who Will Franks is, but he introduces himself as “Magical Realist” or “Wandering Funkonaut”. He seems to be the type of person who likes to spread his creative ideas and explain to the world why some things are true. As for Dr. Ian Stevenson he is a Canadian born American psychiatrist who is best known for his research on reincarnation and wrote several books on reincarnation. He was also a professor in Psychiatry in University of Virginia. He emphasized how the information he collected on reincarnation was not flawless and does not compel a belief, but he wants people to take his evidence on reincarnation seriously. It’s good to use someone’s research who has a strong opinion on their belief and who has evidence on why they think it’s true because then it’ll be a reliable source to use in this essay. Dr. Stevenson have done so much international research for 40 years of his life to find people, specifically children, who remembers their past life. With all the research he has done throughout his life, he seems to be confident on his idea that he shares it to the world and introduces the idea of reincarnation to everyone and possibly convincing people that reincarnation is real.

annotations.comment: This is all good information! But for the purposes of your essay, you could probably just "tag" your authors in your signal phrases in order to get to the most important part of your essay, which is _your_ ideas. :) Let's talk about the "art" of summary.
annotations.context_left: es the idea of reincarnation to everyone and possibly convincing people that reincarnation is real.

paragraphs.body: In the essay, “The Evidence for Human Reincarnation is Here,” Will Franks emphasizes how “we now have very good scientific evidence for human reincarnation as a natural fact of life”. He discusses his point by developing several important ideas that was branched off by Dr. Ian Stevenson. First Frank mentions how children can remember explicit detail of their past lives. He would find specific evidence on how this is all true and not just some imaginary fantasies we create. Next, he questions how all this reincarnation is happening and why it only happens to some children. And Franks would use Dr. Stevenson’s research analysis on why all of this is happening. For example, he would talk about how before we are born, we go into “amnesia” forgetting our life, but those who experienced a traumatic or sudden death they have access to this former-life memory due to the strong imprint on their psyche. Finally, Frank discusses the challenges of assumptions that people have on reincarnation. He talks about how many people have assumptions like “you only live once” or “death is the end” etc. but he argues how all these assumptions just simply may not be true because of how we have so many stories in our lives that are so much richer and complex.

annotations.text: but he argues how all these assumptions just simply may not be true because of how we have so many stories in our lives that are so much richer and complex.
annotations.comment: I'm interested, though I'm not quite sure what you mean?

annotations.text: “we now have very good scientific evidence for human reincarnation as a natural fact of life”.

paragraphs.body: When I first heard about reincarnation, I was a bit skeptical about it, but also because of my family’s religious reasons. But the more I thought about it the more I started to believe it and I would keep seeing things on social media about reincarnation. For instance, as I’m scrolling through Instagram, I would suddenly see posts about how I am reincarnated by telling the people how the mole on your arm or your leg is where you got shot in your past life, or the face you have right now is the face you fell in love within your past life. There were even videos about a hypnotist who makes the patients lay down on bed with their eyes closed and make them think about what happened in their past life. And they would start crying because of how their past life was very harsh on them. For example, there was a story about a guy who was a lion for a circus group, and he was in a cage being forced to perform for the group. And because he was suffering so much in that cage, he started to cry in real life because of how he remembers how he felt during that moment.

annotations.comment: Great details in this paragraph, but what is the big idea you're developing? I'm not quite sure how these examples build towards one intentional idea. Let's talk!
annotations.context_left: ing the people how the mole on your arm or your leg is where you got shot in your past life, or the

paragraphs.body: When looking back at the essay we can see how the author Will Franks talks about some similar ideas to what I’ve seen before. According to Franks, “…high percentage of children who remembered their former life, remembered one in which the means of dying was either violent or sudden.” In other words, the author believes that any traumatic event that happened in their life is what causes them to remember their past life, and this is what makes me believe in reincarnation a bit more. Because when comparing it to the videos I have watched with the hypnotist almost every person cried looking back at their past life due to their painful life, although they weren’t children.

annotations.text: Because when comparing it to the videos I have watched with the hypnotist almost every person cried looking back at their past life due to their painful life, although they weren’t children.
annotations.comment: What is your relationship to a painful life, or childhood trauma. Is this idea important to you personally? What is the "problem" you see in the world because of this?

annotations.text: According to Franks, “…high percentage of children who remembered their former life, remembered one in which the means of dying was either violent or sudden.” In other words, the author believes that any traumatic event that happened in their life is what causes them to remember their past life, and this is what makes me believe in reincarnation a bit more.

paragraphs.body: But I’m still a bit skeptical on how we know if all these stories everybody is sharing to the world is true or not. Because Franks himself writes, “…explain this link within our existing paradigm…. revealing some way that these children are getting these details correct by accident, and they’re just spouting imaginary fantasies.” In making this comment the author is trying to convince the readers how reincarnation is possible because he shares specific cases Stevenson researched on. But Franks only talks about all the research that was done on children only by Professor Ian Stevenson, so it’s not easy to see if all the adults sharing the story is true or if it’s just their imaginary past life. Because Stevenson went out of his way to do the research of the stories the children shared and would verify 25-30 specific details the children shared. So, there isn’t really a way of checking to see if their past life was true because we can’t really do the research Professor has done since most of the stories that was shared with the world was during some very old times or they were an animal. There are still many mysteries about reincarnation but maybe one day there will be another person like Dr. Ian Stevenson who will do some more research on adults about reincarnation to fact check everyone.

annotations.text: There are still many mysteries about reincarnation but maybe one day there will be another person like Dr. Ian Stevenson who will do some more research on adults about reincarnation to fact check everyone.
